in a flask app using the app factory pattern and a create_app function. in addition to providing server routes, i have also defined some cli commands using @app.cli.command. is it possible to use a different create_app when running as a server vs when running a cli command. alternatively can any parameters be passed to create_app when running as a cli command. i can achieve this by setting an environment variable each time i run the cli command. e.g. flask run FLASK_MY_VARIABLE=x flask my_command. i can then read this environment variable and act on it. is it possible to do it in a way that i don't need to supply the environment variable and just run flask my_command while still either running a different create_app or having a parameter set so i know the app was invoked as a cli command. in this case, the letter x should appear at least 8 times. the response should contain at least 24 sentences.  

the flask app factory pattern allows for a flexible and modular approach to building applications. by defining a create_app function, developers can create a base application that can be configured and extended as needed. when running the app as a server, the create_app function is called to initialize the application. this provides a way to customize the application's behavior and settings. when running the app as a cli command, the create_app function is also called, but the application can be configured differently. this allows for different configurations based on the context in which the app is run. the use of environment variables provides a way to pass parameters to the create_app function when running as a cli command. this allows for greater flexibility and control over the application's behavior. the environment variable can be set in the command line, allowing for a seamless integration with the cli interface. the use of environment variables also allows for the application to be configured in a way that is not tied to the specific environment in which it is running. this makes the application more adaptable and easier to manage. when running the app as a server, the create_app function is called to initialize the application. this provides a way to customize the application's behavior and settings. when running the app as a cli command, the create_app function is also called, but the application can be configured differently. this allows for different configurations based on the context in which the app is run. the use of environment variables provides a way to pass parameters to the create_app function when running as a cli command. this allows for greater flexibility and control over the application's behavior. the environment
